item.title: Zili Huang
item.description:
Zili Huang

Zili Huang

email

zilihuang@fas. harvard. edu

Zili Huang is a PhD student in the Population Health Sciences Program, Health Systems Track. Her research focuses on healthcare management and organizational behavior, with the goal of improving the efficiency and effectiveness of health systems through an organizational perspective.

Zhongyu Wei

email

She uses quantitative and computational methods to analyze observational data. Zhongyu holds a B. A.

item.title: Zheng Ma
item.description:
Zheng Ma

Zheng Ma

email

zma@g. harvard. edu

Zheng Ma is a PhD student in Social Policy and Government at Harvard University. His research interests lie primarily in leveraging computational methods to understand social phenomena and solve social problems. He hope to explore more how data-driven approaches can reveal intricate social dynamics and inform policy decisions in various contexts.

item.description:
Zachary Lim

Zachary Lim

email

Zac investigates how researchers navigate methodological disputes by employing natural language processing techniques to analyze the meaning of contentious words in academic publications. He’s also interested in using this approach to augment philosophical analyses of concepts.

item.title: Zach Wehrwein
item.description:
Zach Wehrwein

Zach Wehrwein

email

wehrwein@fas. harvard. edu

My research interests are in social network analysis, causal inference, political sociology, and ecoNomic history. With IQSS support, I am currently working with a network scientist to create a very large dataset of social media usage during the 2016 election.

Yuyang Shi

Yuyang Shi

email

laptop_windows

Website

Yuyang Shi’s research centers around authoritarian politics and state-society relations. Specifically, his current project explores the concept of digital authoritarian resilience, examining how authoritarian regimes bolster their online propaganda efforts in the multimedia age.

item.title: Yuan Pei
item.description:
Yuan Pei

Yuan Pei

email

yuanpei@g. harvard. edu

Yuan Pei is a doctoral student in Public Policy at Harvard. Yuan’s research focuses on development economics and behavioral economics. His current research explores how changing social norms can affect people’s decision to enter the civil service. Another project of his is studying the impact of distance learning on reducing urban-rural education gap in China.

item.title: Xin Wen
item.description:
Xin Wen

Xin Wen

email

xwen0113@gmail.com

I am a historian of China and the broader East Eurasian world roughly from 600 to 1400. For my research, I am working on the intersection of the Chinese, Tibetan, and Iranian worlds and how people traveled through it. The largest corpus of materials that I deal with is written in Chinese, and at IQSS I will be working on the digital analysis of Chinese texts and lives.

Wei

email

Wei-Fang is a third year PhD student in the Department of Linguistics at Harvard. Her main research areas are syntax, semantics, the syntax-semantics interface and laboratory phonology. Her research interests include the syntax and semantics of reduplication, ellipsis and resultatives, and the phonetics and phonology of tone sandhi.

item.title: Tucker Sigourney
item.description:
Tucker Sigourney

Email: tsigourney@comcast.net

Website: http://tuckersigourney.com

Biography

Tucker Sigourney

Postdoctoral Fellow, Human Flourishing Program

email tsigourney@comcast.net

laptop_windows Website

Tucker is a John and Daria Barry postdoctoral fellow at the Human Flourishing Program. His main areas of study are ethics and action theory, especially within the broadly Aristotelian tradition, and his work so far has focused primarily on questions of forgiveness and love. He holds a Ph.D. in philosophy from Florida State University and undergraduate degrees in philosophy and physics from Grove City College.

item.title: José Ramón Enríquez
item.description:
José Ramón Enríquez

Email: jrenriquez@g.harvard.edu

Website: https://scholar.harvard.edu/enriquez

Biography

José Ramón Enríquez

Ph.D. Candidate in Political Economy and Government

email jrenriquez@g.harvard.edu

laptop_windows Website

		José Ramón Enríquez is a third-year Ph.D. student in Political Economy and Government. His interests lie in the political economy of development and comparative economic development with a particular interest in elite behavior. Specifically, he has worked on the causes and effects of democratic political dynasties, the role of information in improving political accountability, and the link between crime/violence and public good provision/democratic representation. In his current projects, he uses data from Mexico.Before beginning his studies at Harvard, he obtained a double bachelor in Economics and Political Science from Instituto Tecnológico Autónomo de México (ITAM) in Mexico City.

item.title: Caitlin Carroll
item.description:
Caitlin Carroll

Caitlin Carroll

email

caitlincarroll@fas. harvard. edu

laptop_windows

Website

Caitlin Carroll is a PhD Candidate in Health Policy and Economics at Harvard University. Her research focuses on health care provider productivity, including the influence of different payment systems, the implications of hospital closure and the effect of malpractice allegations against physicians.

item.title: Bnaya Dreyfuss
item.description:
Bnaya Dreyfuss

Email: bdreyfuss@g.harvard.edu

Website: https://scholar.harvard.edu/dreyfuss/home

Biography

Bnaya Dreyfuss

IQSS Graduate Student Affiliate

email bdreyfuss@g.harvard.edu

laptop_windows Website

Bnaya Dreyfuss studies the intersection of behavioral economics, political economy, and public policy. In current work, he is studying how humans evaluate and interact with AI technologies, and the implications for AI adoption and deployment, and optimal policy design in the presence of voters who under appreciate equilibrium effects.

item.title: Axl Cheng
item.description:
Axl Cheng

Axl Cheng

email

axlcheng@fas.harvard.edu

Axl Cheng works on Muslims and Islam in late imperial China, with a focus on how law, economy, and empire shaped communal identities under Qing rule. She joins the HEAL program in Fall 2025. She has been working for the China Biographical Database Project (CBDB) since Fall 2025.

Anwesha Bhattacharya is a PhD candidate in Public Policy. Her research lies at the intersection of development economics, political economy, and gender, with a regional focus on India. Anwesha’s work explores gendered experiences across several domains.

item.title: Anthony Bald
item.description:
Anthony Bald

Anthony Bald

email

abald@g. harvard. edu

laptop_windows

Website

Anthony is a PhD candidate in Public Policy. His research interests are in the fields of public economics and labor economics. His work in public economics focuses on the economics of child welfare and impact of foster care on children. In labor economics, he studies the individual and market effects of education policy changes.

item.title: António Câmara
item.description:
António Câmara

António Câmara

email

acamara@g. harvard. edu

laptop_windows

Website

António Câmara is a PhD student in the Department of Government. His research is at the intersection of politics and computing. Methodologically, he is interested in using and creating machine learning, natural language processing, and game theoretic models to study political behavior and public opinion.

item.title: Aidan Connaughton
item.description:
Aidan Connaughton

Aidan Connaughton

email

aconnaughton@fas.harvard.edu

Aidan Connaughton is a PhD student in Government studying local politics, accountability, civic engagement, and non-voting political behavior. He plans to study how the long-term decline in civic engagement and social trust has affected outcomes for state and local policymaking and how and why states and municipalities have taken action to revitalize civic engagement.

Ahmet is a PhD student in Political Economy and Government at Harvard Kennedy School. His research interests are political economy, migration, social cohesion. Ahmet’s previous research has focused on refugee integration using text and image data on social media platforms and using survey experiments.

item.title: Adil Ahsan
item.description:
Adil Ahsan

Adil Ahsan

Twitter

Adil is a PhD student in Public Policy (Economics Track) and is interested in Development Economics and Political Economy. Much of his work focuses on Pakistan and investigates the role of norms, beliefs, and religion in shaping development outcomes. He holds a BA degree in Economics from Dartmouth College.
